---
title: EARS-Net
alias: &ALIAS earsnet
href: https://www.ecdc.europa.eu/en/antimicrobial-resistance/surveillance-and-disease-data/ears-net
listing:
  id: pubs
  template: ../../../../_ejs/publications-people.ejs 
  contents: 
    - "../../../../publications/**/*.qmd"
    - "!../../../../publications/_template/"
  sort: "pub_number desc"
  include:
    categories: *ALIAS
  fields: [publication, title, categories, image, date, author]

---

## Overview

The European Antimicrobial Resistance Surveillance Network (EARS-Net, ECDC) is a European network providing reference antimicrobial resistance data for public health purposes across EU/EEA countries.

## Key Features

- Collects standardized susceptibility testing data from clinical isolates across 30+ EU/EEA countries
- Covers priority pathogens including E. coli, K. pneumoniae, S. aureus, E. faecium, and others
- Provides annual reports with temporal trends, geographic distributions, and country comparisons
- Data available for download via ECDC Surveillance Atlas of Infectious Diseases
- Adheres to EUCAST breakpoints and standardized microbiological methodology across networks

## Use Cases in Research

- Analyzing pan-European AMR prevalence trends for key ESKAPE and priority pathogens
- Comparing national AMR rates to benchmark clinical and surveillance programs
- Generating AMR epidemiological context for genomic and mechanistic resistance studies
- Training predictive models of AMR spread using longitudinal European surveillance data
- Informing antibiotic stewardship and treatment guideline development with evidence-based resistance rates

## Getting Started

Visit [EARS-Net](https://www.ecdc.europa.eu/en/antimicrobial-resistance/surveillance-and-disease-data/ears-net) to access the database.

## Related Publications

:::{#pubs}
:::
